# GdUnit4

> A Godot 4 embedded unit testing framework (plugin) supporting GDScript and C#.

GdUnit4 v6.1.2 — supports Godot 4.3–4.6. C# targets net9.0 (.NET SDK 9.0.308). The framework is self-tested: it uses itself to run its own test suite. The plugin lives entirely under `addons/gdUnit4/`.

**License:** MIT

Keywords: Godot unit testing, GDScript testing framework, Godot 4 plugin, game testing, test-driven development, TDD, Godot C# testing, automated testing, unit test, integration test, scene testing, signal testing, mock, spy, stub, fuzzing, parameterized tests, CI/CD, GitHub Actions, JUnit XML, HTML report

## Why GdUnit4

GdUnit4 is a feature-rich testing framework for Godot 4, designed to make writing and running tests enjoyable for both GDScript and C# developers.

- **Dual-language support** — GDScript and C# in a single framework
- **Fluent assertion API** — chainable, type-aware assertions with descriptive failure messages
- **Full mock/stub/spy support** — isolate dependencies and verify interactions
- **Scene Runner** — simulate user input (keyboard, mouse, actions) on live nodes/UI in tests
- **Signal assertions** — await and assert signal emissions with timeout support
- **Fuzzing / parameterized tests** — data-driven test cases with built-in fuzzers
- **CI/CD ready** — JUnit XML and HTML reports, headless mode, GitHub Actions integration
- **Godot editor integration** — Test Inspector panel with run/re-run, green/red tree, filtering
- **Test hooks** — `before`/`after` (suite-level) and `before_test`/`after_test` (per-test) setup and teardown callbacks
- **Session hooks** — global setup and teardown logic that runs once per test session via `GdUnitTestSessionHook`
- **Integrated update tool** — check for and install new releases directly from the Godot editor
- **Asset Library** — installable directly from the Godot Editor Asset Library

## Installation

**Via Godot Asset Library** (recommended): Search for "GdUnit4" in the Godot Editor → AssetLib tab.

**Via release download**: Download the latest `.zip` from [GitHub releases](https://github.com/MikeSchulze/gdUnit4/releases), extract into your project's `addons/` folder, then enable the plugin in *Project → Project Settings → Plugins*.

## Quick Start (GDScript)

```gdscript
extends GdUnitTestSuite


func test_string_contains() -> void:
    assert_str("Hello GdUnit4").contains("GdUnit4")


func test_array_has_size() -> void:
    assert_array([1, 2, 3]).has_size(3).contains(1, 2)


func test_signal_emitted() -> void:
    var timer := auto_free(Timer.new())
    add_child(timer)
    timer.start(0.1)
    await assert_signal(timer).is_emitted("timeout")


func test_mock_example() -> void:
    var mock_node := mock(Node)
    mock_node.get_name()
    verify(mock_node).get_name()


func test_scene_runner() -> void:
    # load a scene and wrap it in a runner
    var runner := scene_runner("res://scenes/MyGame.tscn")
    # simulate a key press and advance frames
    runner.simulate_key_pressed(KEY_SPACE)
    await runner.simulate_frames(30)
    # assert expected scene state
    assert_object(runner.find_child("Player")).is_not_null()
```

## Quick Start (C#)

```csharp
using GdUnit4;
using static GdUnit4.Assertions;

[TestSuite]
public class MyTest
{
    [TestCase]
    public void StringContains()
        => AssertThat("Hello GdUnit4").Contains("GdUnit4");

    [TestCase]
    public void ArrayHasSize()
        => AssertThat(new[] { 1, 2, 3 }).HasSize(3);

    // Parameterized test — each [TestCase] attribute is one run
    [TestCase("foo", 3)]
    [TestCase("hello", 5)]
    public void StringLength(string value, int expectedLength)
        => AssertThat(value).HasLength(expectedLength);
}
```

For C# signal assertions, mocking, and scene runner see the [documentation site](https://godot-gdunit-labs.github.io/gdUnit4/latest/).

## Organisation

GdUnit4 is part of the **[godot-gdunit-labs](https://github.com/godot-gdunit-labs)** GitHub organisation, which bundles all related projects:

- **[GdUnit4](https://github.com/godot-gdunit-labs/gdUnit4)** — the core Godot plugin (GDScript + C# via GDNative bindings)
- **[GdUnit4Net](https://github.com/godot-gdunit-labs/gdUnit4Net)** — standalone .NET / C# test adapter and NuGet package
- **[gdUnit4NetExamples](https://github.com/godot-gdunit-labs/gdUnit4NetExamples)** — example projects showing C# test setup and patterns
- **[gdUnit4-action](https://github.com/godot-gdunit-labs/gdUnit4-action)** — GitHub Action for running GdUnit4 tests in CI pipelines

## Docs

- [Documentation site](https://godot-gdunit-labs.github.io/gdUnit4/latest/): Full reference — assertions, mocking, spying, scene runner, fuzzing, CI setup
- [GitHub organisation](https://github.com/godot-gdunit-labs): All related repositories
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/MikeSchulze/gdUnit4/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md): Contribution guidelines
- [Discord server](https://discord.gg/rdq36JwuaJ): Community support and general discussion
- [Patreon](https://www.patreon.com/cw/GodotGdUnitLabs): Support the project

## Running Tests (CLI)

```bash
# Set Godot binary, then run all tests
export GODOT_BIN=/path/to/godot
./addons/gdUnit4/runtest.sh

# Run a specific path
./addons/gdUnit4/runtest.sh --godot_binary /path/to/godot -a res://addons/gdUnit4/test/asserts/
```

## Plugin Structure

```
addons/gdUnit4/
├── plugin.cfg          # Plugin metadata and version
├── plugin.gd           # EditorPlugin entry point
├── bin/                # CLI tools (GdUnitCmdTool.gd, GdUnitCopyLog.gd)
├── src/                # All framework source code
└── test/               # Self-tests (mirrors src/ structure)
```

For full source module breakdown, coding conventions, and contributor guidelines see [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt).
